title: 10 best herbal decoctions for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for swollen gums are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a concentrated liquid solution that can be used to soothe and calm inflamed and irritated gum tissue. These decoctions help to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and combat bacterial infections that cause gum disease. Examples of herbal decoctions that provide relief include chamomile tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes healing, and clove oil, which has antibacterial properties that eliminate harmful bacteria. By using these natural remedies, individuals can improve their oral health, reducing discomfort and improving overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenes work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for treating gingivitis and periodontal disease. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting healthy gums and a reduction in swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains calyx, which has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for swollen gums.

Antimicrobial: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in pot marigold decoctions exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum infection and inflammation.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds help protect gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate swelling and pain in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Barks: The barks contain salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that can help soothe gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean fresh calendula officinalis flowers according to organic farming standards for optimal potency.
Measure and add one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or two teaspoons of fresh calendula flowers per 8 ounces boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan over medium heat for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reaches a simmering temperature.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids after five minutes of steeping time.
Allow the cooled calendula decoction to sit at room temperature for two hours before using as an oral rinse to alleviate swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen decoctions helps with swollen gums because it contains salivan, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that effectively reduces swelling and pain. The decoction's bioactive compounds also exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the gum inflammation. Additionally, danshen decoctions contain antioxidants that promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and promoting collagen synthesis, thereby facilitating the repair of damaged tissues in the gums. By addressing the root causes of swollen gums, danshen decoctions provide natural relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salvianolic acid and danshensu, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by suppressing inflammation.

Antimicrobial property: The decoction's antimicrobial properties, courtesy of compounds like saikosaponin and cinnamic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and promote healing.

Vasodilatory property: Danshen decoctions contain vasodilators like protocatechuic aldehyde, which help to improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the gums, thereby accelerating healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to treat swelling and pain in the gum due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort caused by swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 9-12 grams of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root into a heat-resistant container.
Combine the measured root with 6-8 cups of distilled water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer for 30-40 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract fully.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and then store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort. The flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating inflammation in the gums. Additionally, licorice decoctions contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been found to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which helps to combat infections and promote healing. This natural remedy can provide relief from swollen gums, promoting oral health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice decoctions contain triterpenoids like glycyrrhizin, which reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root has antimicrobial compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat infections that can cause gum swelling and inflammation.
Soften and protect mucous membranes: Licorice's saponins help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing discomfort and swelling associated with gum conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used in traditional medicine due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and soothes gum swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is the underground stem of the plant, contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a heat-resistant glass container.
Combine the measured powder with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan and stir well.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with swollen gums because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active ingredients, such as echinacein and chlorogenic acid, work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the affected area. This natural remedy effectively soothes and calms swollen gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with gum infections and inflammation. By reducing swelling and inflammation, Kansas coneflower decoctions promote healthy gum tissue and overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like isoquercetin and kaempferol, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, can help to combat infections that contribute to swollen gums.

Wound-healing properties: Kansas coneflower contains saponins, which have been shown to promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue integrity, and reducing inflammation, making it helpful in treating gum lesions and ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen gums.
Barks: The bark of the Kansas coneflower contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help shrink swollen tissues and reduce bleeding in gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall, selecting roots that are at least one year old for optimal potency.
Clean the harvested echinacea angustifolia roots thoroughly under cold running water, removing any dirt or debris.
Cut the cleaned echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces to increase their surface area and facilitate infusion.
Combine 1 cup of the cut echinacea angustifolia roots with 2 cups of boiling distilled water in a large glass jar, letting it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The antioxidants present in dandelion help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate saliva production can wash away bacteria and food particles that can contribute to gum irritation. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy mouth environment, dandelion decoctions can provide relief from swollen gums, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and polyphenols that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, which can contribute to gum disease and swollen gums.
Astringent properties: Dandelion's astringent properties help reduce bleeding and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, including inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe irritated gums and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gently boil 2 teaspoons of dried dandelion root in 4 cups of water for 10-15 minutes.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for an additional 20-30 minutes to enhance flavonoids.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the liquid to cool down to room temperature within 1 hour before consumption.
Consume 2 cups of the cooled decoction as needed, up to three times daily for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with swollen gums because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a decoction, goldenseal's berberine works to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause inflammation. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation. As a result, goldenseal decoctions can provide effective relief from swollen gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate gum inflammation by combating bacteria and reducing infection.
Berbamine: Exerts a vasoconstrictive effect, causing blood vessels to constrict and reduce swelling in the affected area, thereby relieving symptoms of swollen gums.
Coptisine: Has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help soothe and calm irritated gum tissue, reducing redness and swelling associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that helps combat bacterial infections causing swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder or one tablespoon of fresh root into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of pure water in a teapot or kettle and let it cool for 5 minutes to prevent burning.
Pour the boiling water over the measured hydrastis canadensis powder in the cup and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before using it as a mouthwash or gargle three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the mouth. Additionally, sweet cicely has natural antibacterial properties that help combat the underlying infections causing gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and combating bacteria, sweet cicely decoctions provide a natural remedy for soothing swollen gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Sweet cicely decoction has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gums, which helps alleviate swelling and pain associated with gum disease.
Antimicrobial property: The decoction's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that cause gum disease, reducing the severity of swollen gums and promoting healing.
Analgesic property: Sweet cicely contains compounds that have analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds contain flavonoids that have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat underlying infections causing swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried myrrhis odorata herb for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured herb with cold filtered water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches 200°f and 93°c.
Allow the decoction to steep for 30-40 minutes then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and use the resulting liquid as an oral rinse up to four times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease pain. The antioxidants present in elderberries help to neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation, while the bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This natural remedy also exhibits antibacterial properties, helping to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to swollen gums. Regular consumption of elder decoctions can provide relief from gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry's anthocyanins and quercetin have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.

Antimicrobial: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in elderberry decoctions have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to swollen gums.
Antioxidant: Elderberry's rich antioxidant content can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make a soothing decoction that helps reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with swollen gums.
Bark: The bark contains compounds that have antibacterial properties, which can help combat infections and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Rinse sambucus nigra berries thoroughly with distilled water to remove any debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves with one cup boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
Use 1/2 teaspoon of the cooled decoction three times daily as a mouthwash by swishing it around your mouth for one minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its rich content of silica, a mineral that has anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction absorbs into the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and swelling by neutralizing acidic compounds and promoting healthy collagen production. Additionally, field horsetail's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation, leading to a reduction in redness, pain, and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: Horsetail has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria that can cause gum disease, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.

Wound-healing properties: Horsetail's high silica content can help stimulate collagen production, which is essential for wound healing and tissue repair in the gums, promoting a healthy and firm gingival attachment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in silica and antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 teaspoons of dried equisetum arvense herb with cold water and soak overnight in 4 cups of boiling water for 8 hours.
Drain and discard the solids, then add 2 teaspoons of fresh ginger root and boil the liquid for an additional 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use, using 1/2 cup as needed to rinse and soothe swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile work synergistically to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation caused by gum disease or irritation from dental procedures. As a natural antiseptic, chamomile decoctions also help eliminate bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects calm the gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling in gum tissues by decreasing prostaglandins' production, a hormone responsible for inflammation.
Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial activity inhibits the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing of swollen gums.

Anxiolytic and calming effect: Chamomile's soothing properties help calm nervousness and anxiety, which are common underlying factors for swollen gums (e.g., bruxism or teeth grinding). By reducing stress and anxiety, chamomile decoctions promote relaxation and alleviate gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used to make decoctions as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and ease pain in gum infections.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in antioxidants, which can help soothe and calm the inflamed tissues of the gums, reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and add it to one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the residue.
Store the prepared decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to two days.
Use the decoction as needed by gargling with one-quarter cup of the liquid four times daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal decoctions is to prepare a warm infusion of calendula flowers, chamomile, and peppermint leaves. Boil 1 teaspoon of dried herbs in 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. Use this tea as a mouthwash by swishing it around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Repeat twice daily for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal decoctions are various oral health issues, such as periodontitis, pyorrhea, and thrush. Decoctions of herbs like neem, turmeric, and clove have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected areas. Additionally, decoctions made from ingredients like sage, chamomile, and peppermint can soothe gum inflammation and freshen breath. These herbal remedies can provide effective relief without harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
